name: claude-code-cockpit
description: Local-first HUD for Claude Code. Token + cost rollups (session/day/week/month/year), cross-project session Watchtower, Mac/Obsidian integrations, 30+ customizable widgets per tab. Read-only. 100% local.

commands.title: Claude Cockpit: Open Active Session JSONL
Claude Cockpit: Save Session to Obsidian
Claude Cockpit: Set Daily Budget Cap
Claude Cockpit: Install Skill from URL
Claude Cockpit: Save Current Tab Layout As…
Claude Cockpit: Pop Out Fullscreen Grid
Claude Cockpit: Approve All Pending (Cockpit-source only)
Claude Cockpit: Reject All Pending (Cockpit-source only)
Claude Cockpit: Revert Last Snapshotted Action
Claude Cockpit: Reveal Replay Forks Folder
Claude Cockpit: Copy Mobile Public Queue Path
Claude Cockpit: Open Mobile Companion Setup

claudeCockpit.budget.enabled.description: Enable budget alerts when daily/session caps are exceeded.

claudeCockpit.budget.dailyCapUsd.description: Daily spend cap in USD across all projects. 0 disables the daily cap.

claudeCockpit.budget.sessionCapUsd.description: Per-session spend cap in USD. 0 disables the session cap.

claudeCockpit.budget.weeklyCapUsd.description: Weekly spend cap in USD (Mon-Sun). 0 disables the weekly cap. Drives the Usage rollups widget.

claudeCockpit.budget.monthlyCapUsd.description: Monthly spend cap in USD (calendar month). 0 disables the monthly cap. Drives the Usage rollups widget.

claudeCockpit.budget.yearlyCapUsd.description: Yearly spend cap in USD (calendar year). 0 disables the yearly cap. Drives the Usage rollups widget.

claudeCockpit.cloudRoutines.enabled.description: Surface a deep-link to manage cloud routines (scheduled remote agents) on claude.ai. Off by default to preserve Cockpit's local-only stance — turning this on does not yet make any network calls; it just unhides the link.

claudeCockpit.theme.description: Initial theme for the Cockpit sidebar. 'auto' inherits VSCode's theme via CSS variables (default). 'dark'/'light' force a fixed palette regardless of VSCode's theme. 'high-contrast' uses a pure-black palette tuned for low-vision and high-glare environments (every text/UI pair meets WCAG AA, most meet AAA). The Customize panel inside the sidebar can override this at runtime.

claudeCockpit.discover.enabled.description: Enable the Discover tab (top GitHub projects + RSS from Obsidian). Off by default — turning it on lets Cockpit fetch api.github.com on demand (when you click Refresh). RSS reads from your Obsidian vault and makes no network calls.

claudeCockpit.wisprShortcut.description: Optional. Your Wispr Flow keyboard shortcut so Cockpit's Talk tab can trigger dictation. Format: 'control+option+space'. Empty = no synthetic trigger; you press the shortcut yourself. macOS only.

claudeCockpit.updateCheck.enabled.description: Periodically check api.github.com/repos/sboghossian/claude-code-cockpit/releases/latest for new releases of this extension and surface an 'Update available' pill in the header. Set to false for fully local operation with zero update-check traffic.

claudeCockpit.roadmap.enabled.description: Allow the Roadmap tab to fetch project metadata from roadmap.dashable.dev (or localhost:3000 if running the roadmap server locally). On by default — the endpoint is owned by the same operator as this extension. Set to false to make the Roadmap tab show only cached data (under ~/.claude/.cache/cockpit-roadmap.json) with no outbound traffic.

claudeCockpit.surfaces.macHealth.description: Show the Mac Health tab (disk / memory / battery / CPU / Wi-Fi / Bluetooth peripherals). Disable to hide the tab and skip the periodic system_profiler probe (~1s every 30s). Cockpit's wedge is Claude observability, not iStat — keep this off if you already use a dedicated system monitor.

claudeCockpit.surfaces.appUsage.description: Track which macOS app is in focus once per minute (cheap, local-only) and render today's hourly bar chart. Disable to skip the lsappinfo poll and hide the App Usage panel.

claudeCockpit.surfaces.subdomainHealth.description: HEAD-probe the always-live subdomains in your PILOT memory every 60s so the dots reflect reality. Disable for fully local operation with zero outbound HEAD requests.

claudeCockpit.audit.enabled.description: Append meta-events (outbound network calls, key-add/delete) to ~/.claude/.cockpit/audit.log. Local-only; never exfiltrated. Drives the Audit, Outbound, and Keys sub-views in the Security tab. Disable for zero-write operation — existing log files are preserved but no new events are appended.

claudeCockpit.approval.autoSnapshot.description: Capture a pre-action filesystem snapshot of `WorktreeAction.filesAffected` when an entry lands in the approval queue, so revert is possible. Off = the queue still surfaces actions but revert is unavailable.

claudeCockpit.approval.snapshotMaxBytes.description: Per-action snapshot size cap in bytes (default 50 MB). Captures larger than this are rejected and the entry is marked snapshot-failed; the action is not blocked.

claudeCockpit.approval.requireForToolNam.description: Hook-side hint: which Claude Code tool names should be routed through the approval queue. Cockpit itself does NOT enforce — wire your PreToolUse hook to read this list (v1.1 will ship the hook).

claudeCockpit.replay.maxEventsPerSession.description: Maximum events the Replay tab loads per session. Larger values give finer scrub granularity but cost more memory + CPU on initial parse. Above 5000 the scrubber uniformly samples to keep the slider responsive.

claudeCockpit.notifications.enabled.description: Show desktop notifications when the approval queue gets new entries (0 → 1), an audit event is blocked, or today's spend crosses 80% of the daily cap. Each notification is debounced 30s per key.

claudeCockpit.mobile.enabled.description: Publish a SANITIZED, read-only snapshot of the approval queue to ~/.claude/.cockpit/queue.public.json so a phone can render it via your existing Cloudflare Tunnel + Cloudflare Access SSO. Cockpit makes ZERO outbound calls — you serve the file yourself. Default off. The published payload contains only: id, tool, ageSeconds, agentName (≤8 chars), expectedDiffBytes, status, fileCount. NO file paths, NO arguments, NO secrets. v1.0 is read-only; mobile-side approve lands in v1.1.

claudeCockpit.telemetry.enabled.description: Opt-in usage analytics via PostHog. Default OFF — Cockpit is 100% local until you flip this. When enabled, Cockpit captures aggregate counters (tab views, command invocations, session duration) and a redacted stack on uncaught extension errors. Detail is redacted before send: no file paths, no prompt text, no session content, no API keys. All HTTP goes through the extension host (Node), never the webview, and every outbound POST is mirrored to ~/.claude/.cockpit/audit.log so you can SEE what left the machine. Requires claudeCockpit.telemetry.projectId to be set; otherwise this stays a no-op.

claudeCockpit.telemetry.crashReports.description: Opt-in crash reporting. Default OFF. Independently gated from telemetry.enabled — many users want usage counters but not crash stacks (anonymized stack frames can still hint at file system layout). When ON and telemetry.enabled is also ON, uncaught errors in activate() / the webview message handler / wrapped async surfaces are sent to PostHog with home directory replaced by ~ and external (non-Cockpit) frames blanked.

claudeCockpit.telemetry.projectId.description: Your PostHog project id (api_key). Empty = telemetry forced off. Cockpit explicitly refuses project id 92178 (HAQQ Legal AI's customer-telemetry project) so an extension diagnostic never leaks into customer data. To run your own dashboard, create a Cockpit-only PostHog project and paste its id here.

claudeCockpit.telemetry.host.description: PostHog host. Override only if you self-host. Most users should leave this default.
